painting-1.title: Untitled Oil
painting-1.description: Oil on canvas, 2024. Exploring texture and light through layered glazing techniques.

procreate-1.title: Character Sketch
procreate-1.description: Digital illustration on Procreate. Part of an ongoing character design series.

blender-1.title: Blender Scene
blender-1.description: 3D render in Blender. Experimenting with volumetric lighting and subsurface scattering.

IMG_0325.title: Perched
IMG_0325.description: Perched is a simple animal study I did of a Red-Billed Leothrix perched on a branch. The Red Billed Leothrix is a bird native to the Himalayan area, and I have fond memories of the bright birds’ chirping from when I went hiking in that mountain range. I used soft pastels for the entire piece, and it’s of A3 size- 29.7*42 cm.

IMG_0330.title: Ticking
IMG_0330.description: Ticking is a poster I created about pollution and over- industrialization. I wanted to keep it minimalist and make the message clear, so I used clean, simple lines, kept the background plain and used a short phrase that encapsulates what I feel about our window to clean up the Earth- that Time is Ticking, and we’re losing our opportunity to save the Earth for future generations. The poster is meant as a warning, and to incite active change for measures against pollution.

IMG_0331.title: Still Life (graphite)
IMG_0331.description: This is a simple observational piece of objects in my kitchen I did in graphite pencils and black color pencil for the darker sections.

IMG_0333.title: Red Riding Hood
IMG_0333.description: As a child, one of my favorite stories was Red Riding Hood. I hoped to capture the magic of what enticed me in this piece- Red Riding Hood's bright cape set against the gorgeous forest, little animals twittering all around, with the sinister wolf lurking at the bottom adding a little bit of grey darkness (literally and figuratively) to the scene.

IMG_2298.title: Indra's Penance
IMG_2298.description: As a story lover, I have always been interested in mythology. Indra’s Penance is a glass painting depicting a popular Indian myth- the God of Gods, Indra, after committing a sin, is forced to hide underwater from his brethren while an evil demon takes over his kingdom. It’s a story that has always stuck with me- how even the Gods are not absolved of their sins, and they must perform penance to atone for their wrongdoings. In the story, the pivotal focus is Indra hiding in a lotus stem until his subjects beg him to come back, which I painted here on a square glass sheet 30*30 cm.

IMG_3133.title: Rhapsody
IMG_3133.description: I’ve been learning the violin since 2013. It is one of the most intrinsic parts of my personality, something I cannot live without. Rhapsody is a self portrait I created attempting to capture the different emotions I experience when I play. I don’t often express my emotions on my face, so I kept my face serene while the background is bright and awash with color- red for the deeper emotions, black for the intense sadness in some songs, yellow for my happiness. I painted myself a little older and in a gown, because that’s the person I aspire to be, and the person I hope emerges when I play the violin. The piece is 2* 1.5 feet.

IMG_3246.title: Tiruvila
IMG_3246.description: Tiruvila is a Tamil word that means festival. It is a depiction of the quintessential pooja. South Indian culture is extremely specific when it comes to worshipping the gods. No matter the pooja, the items used for worship are the same even if the prayers chanted are different. A typical pooja includes items like utensils, flowers, food and fruit. I created Tiruvila as an observational work during the festival Ganesh Chaturthi, one of the most sacred festivals for people in the state of Karnataka.

Pop_Art.title: Shanaya
Pop_Art.description: Shanaya is a Kannada name that means First Ray of the Sun. When I created this piece, I was very interested in pop art, specifically pop art faces as they have a certain characterisation that I tried to capture in this work. I made this piece on November 1st, when my home state (Karnataka) celebrates its official recognition as a state of India, so the colors (red and yellow) are inspired by the Kannada flag. Shanaya is my imagining of Karnataka as a woman, and the background is reminiscent of Sun’s rays, representing her divinity. It’s a square work of 2048*2048 px.

postcard final.title: The Next Journey
postcard final.description: When COVID-19 began to affect the daily lives of people, Indian railway communication lines really suffered as they were not able to cope with the required protocols to maintain the railways.  So many people lost their only means of travel, and thousands of jobs across India were lost in the process. I wanted this postcard to signify how COVID affected the number of people using railways. For the piece, I used watercolors , in a muted palette- light and dark grey, different shades of blue, red and viridian green for the windows- because I wanted the tone to be slightly melancholic, contrasted by the brightness of the sky outside the station. The inside of the train is also warm and inviting, enticing the figure to step inside and travel.
